title: interview output envelope
description: Envelope returned by `hpc-mapreduce interview`. Reports the artifacts persisted (interview.json, optionally meta.json) and a dry-resolve preview so the calling agent can echo 'sweep starts here, midpoint here, ends here' back to the operator before submit — catching off-by-one and mis-ranged tasks.py at the interview stage rather than after burning compute.

campaign_dir.description: Absolute path to the campaign workdir (where tasks.py and the persisted interview.json live).

artifacts.description: Relative paths (under campaign_dir) of every file the primitive wrote or updated. Always includes interview.json; includes meta.json when intent.cluster_target or intent.budget triggered an update.

total_tasks.description: Result of calling tasks.total() against the campaign's tasks.py. Cross-checked against intent.task_count — mismatch raises spec_invalid before interview.json is written.

cmd_sha.description: compute_cmd_sha() over the campaign's tasks.py. Embedded in interview.json so future cmd_recall queries can detect when an old recipe has drifted from the agent that produced it.

preview.description: Three sample resolve() calls. Each is a dict per claude-hpc's pre-existing tasks.py contract (kwargs get **-unpacked into the user's task function and must be JSON-serializable for cmd_sha). The schema does not constrain the dict's keys — a hyperparameter sweep returns {'lr': 1e-3, 'wd': 1e-2}, an eval campaign returns {'model': ..., 'dataset': ...}, an RL rollout returns {'env': ..., 'seed': ...}.
